rulings.Case Title: DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT VS. ROE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Discovery Dut-Off Regarding Outstanding Subpoenas For Updated Medical And Employment Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 4, 2013, Line 1: Defendant EVENT MAGIC, INC.'S Motion For Relief From Discovery Cut-Off Regarding Outstanding Subpoenas For Updated Medical And Employment Records Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss why discovery should not be reopened in light of new trial date. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TELEB, AELLA A MINOR BY AN THROUGH HER GAL LILIAN et al VS. WINNIE DUNBAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF AELLA TELEB, A MINOR BY AN THROUGH HER GAL LILIAN PEREZ's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Hearing required. It is not clear from the moving papers which clients who are the subject of the motion to withdraw as counsel, and whether each of the affected clients has been served. Specifically, it is not clear whether counsel seeks to withdraw from representing Arnel Teleb. Further, any proposed order must state the name of each client affected by the motion in item 1 (not "et al."), and any order, if signed, would be effective only upon filing proof of service (item 5(a)). Counsel is requested to bring a revised proposed order on the required Judicial Council form that reflects these changes. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TORO MEDIA LLC, et al VS. ASPIRE ROCK LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT ASPIRE ROCK LLC and EASY.LY INC.'s Motion To Stay Or Dismiss. Defendants' motion to dismiss is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. A CCP § 430.10 motion is the proper procedure for enforcing a forum selection clause. (See Cal-State Business Products & Services, Inc. v. Ricoh (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1666, 1680, "a defendant may enforce a forum-selection clause by bringing a motion pursuant to sections 410.30 and 418.10, the statutes governing forum non conveniens motions¿") The forum selection clause is mandatory and therefore, will be given effect unless enforcement would be unreasonable. (Intershop Communications v. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 191, 196.) Plaintiffs have not established that enforcement would be unreasonable. In the alternative, issue preclusion applies to the determination of whether the forum selection clause is reasonable, as all of the factors for issue preclusion enumerated by the California Supreme Court are met here. (Lucido v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 51 Cal.3d 335, 341.) The New York court has already ruled on this issue. Additionally, Defendants did not waive their right to bring this motion, nor is this motion untimely. A reasonableness standard governs the time that a CCP § 410.30 motion must be brought, and Defendants brought this motion within a reasonable time. (See Trident Labs v. Merrill Lynch Comm. Fin. Corp. ((2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 147, 155.) Enforcing the forum selection clause does not violate public policy, as both California and New York recognize the enforceability of forum selection clauses. (See Swenson v. T-Mobile USA, Inc. (S.D. Cal. 2006) 415 F.Supp.2d 1101, 1105, "the question is not whether the application of the forum's law would violate the policy of the other party's state, but rather, whether enforcement of the forum selection agreement would violate the policy of the other party's state as to the forum for litigation of the dispute.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL LEARNING CENTER, DBA THE LAUREL SCHOOL VS. DEBRA SCHOENBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Seal; Stip
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 4, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT DEBRA SCHOENBERG's Motion To Seal; Stipulation. The motion to file the discovery documents that are the subject of this motion under seal, and to substitute redacted copies in the public file that identify the minors as "L.S." and "B. Schoenberg" is granted, for good cause shown. No opposition was filed. This order applies to the following documents: Debra R. Schoenberg's Separate Statement of Items in Dispute, filed July 29, 2013; and Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents Set One and for Attorney Fees and Costs (filed July 29, 2013). These documents shall be sealed, and redacted copies shall replace these pleadings in the public file. Counsel for moving party must provide this Order and redacted copies to the clerk's office. This order does not apply to any other documents in this action. Further, in their Stipulation for Motion to Seal, filed August 19, 2013, the parties have stipulated that the minor child who is mentioned in Cross-Complainant's pleading shall be identified as "L.S." in this action. Based on the stipulation, it is so ordered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss whether documents are available from other sources, and if not, issues concerning a protective order. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
